sessions.title: Substrate Architecture, Hypergraphs, and Modular Design
sessions.summary: Session focused on graph formalism decisions for the Data Transformation System. Key decisions made and recorded.
decisions: Hybrid representation model (leaf attributes) rejected — counter to design philosophy. Complexity absorbed upward through hierarchy.
Base substrate: directed graphs, binary edge types (structural/operational), no node typing, no weights in prototype.

Binary edge typing accepted: two types only, globally fixed. Structural edges define patterns, operational edges define transformations. Does not clutter rule space. Reduces isomorphism complexity via regime-separated matching.
Modular substrate approach adopted: start with base graphs as prototype, upgrade paths (hyperedges, weights) evaluated empirically against measured failures.
Substrate upgrades are manual researcher decisions at training start — not automated.
Two-level evaluation principle: every substrate decision evaluated for both the traveler (object level) and the second system (meta level).
Second system explicitly noted: represents traveler as graph, evolves it via graph rewriting, uses same substrate and interface. Design deliberately deferred.
Graph Operation Interface defined: five operation groups — structural query, transformation, compression evaluation, library interaction, substrate introspection.
open_questions_updated: q_06 expanded to hypergraphs and rule firing
q_09 layer hierarchy compensation for isomorphism cost
sessions.risk_assessment: Isomorphism cost is the primary technical risk. Second system stability is the primary design risk. Scope management is the primary execution risk. User's expectations calibrated — high risk, high reward, modular approach is correct mitigation.
sessions.memory_system_updates: session_loader upgraded to v0.6.3 with bold loading, online/offline model, context eviction. All index files updated with metadata (importance, tags, last_updated). Comparative memory solutions documented in notes.drafts d_01 with open-source optimization candidates.
sessions.project_setup: Project Perspective claude.ai project instructions drafted for future session entry point.

sessions.title: Layer Structure Correction, GA Fitness Signal, and Prototype Roadmap
sessions.summary: Session corrected a documentation error in the layer structure framing, established the GA fitness signal, resolved q_02 and q_04, reframed q_07-q_09 as empirical decision gates, and produced the first prototype roadmap.
decisions: Layer structure framing corrected: layer identity is determined by representational scale (what a node represents), not by operation type. Reversibility is a system-wide constraint of the Data Transformation System, not a property distinguishing layers from each other. Previous framing (middle layers = reversible, upper layers = subgraph replacement) was a neural network projection error — not derived from the design.

Layer boundary definition established: a subgraph from layer N becomes a node at layer N+1 when the GA has converged on rules that consistently produce that subgraph for a given label. Boundaries are earned through convergence, not assigned.

GA is load-bearing for the prototype: without rule improvement, label-conditioned reproducibility of subgraph production is not achievable. The empirical library cannot populate meaningfully from fixed rules.

Fitness signal defined: given a fixed label, minimize variance in subgraph structures produced across different images with that label. Lower variance = higher fitness. Library entry is a byproduct of GA convergence — not a prerequisite for it. Cold start circularity resolved.
q_02 resolved: compression criterion is label-conditioned reproducibility under converged rules.
q_04 partially resolved: fitness signal for GA defined. Self-evolution mechanism deferred to second system.
q_07, q_08, q_09 reframed: not pre-roadmap blockers. They are empirical decision gates answered by Phase 6 of the prototype roadmap.
Prototype roadmap created (d_02): 6 phases plus Phase 5b for minimal GA. Scope bounded to MNIST base prototype.
sessions.contamination_check: Full knowledge base scan performed. Neural network layer framing was not persisted in any chunk — it was a live generation error only. No repair needed beyond the frameworks chunk correction made this session.
open_decisions_flagged: Quantization scheme for pixel-to-graph encoding (Phase 2 of roadmap).
Initial rule set for burn-in before GA activates (Phase 5b of roadmap).
